title: Contract Tests, Race and Leak Checks, Chaos, and Release · Runtime Contract: Fault Injection Matrix
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must layer pure tests, fuzzing, race and leak checks, disposable-broker faults, compatible rollout, upgrade, rollback, and recovery proof; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the fault injection matrix increment in a production Go messaging defense dossier. Learn…
objectives: Inject return, nack, channel close, heartbeat loss, blocked connection, duplicate, poison, dependency timeout, majority loss, restart, stale generation, race, and goroutine-leak failures.

Test pure policy, envelope schema, topology routes, publisher confirms, consumer effects, disposable broker integration, and production probes at distinct layers; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Fault Injection Matrix · Theory Fault Injection Matrix
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 8312 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate release on contracts, race and leak evidence, broker faults, compatibility, rollback, and upgrade path; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then remove the parking binding and expose the remediation gap. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Inject return, nack, channel close, heartbeat loss, blocked connection, duplicate, poison, dependency timeout, majority loss, restart, stale generation, race, and goroutine-leak failures.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Fault Injection Matrix contract for the supplied data.
